results.possibleIdentity: Thomas J. Gengo was an electrical engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, primarily associated with M. & T. Chemicals Inc. (also known as Metal & Thermit Corporation) in New Jersey. His technical expertise was in the fields of power control and plating supplies, reflecting M. & T. Chemicals' specialty in electroplating and chemical production. Gengo's professional details, including his employment at M. & T. Chemicals and his residency/work locations in Matawan and Bricktown, New Jersey, are documented in the archives of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.). He was a participant in E.A.T.'s Technical Services Program, which utilized a database of engineers (recorded on edge-notched index cards) to match technical experts with artists for collaborative projects. He is likely the same Thomas J. Gengo (also known as Joseph Thomas Gengo) who later resided in Mobile, Alabama, and became a donor to the University of South Alabama.
